title: hieracium pilosella subsp. velutinum
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. velutinum, also known as mouse-ear hawkweed or downy hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium pilosella plant, a flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium pilosella subsp. velutinum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
